_comment: Compound-class flags for NOAEL gate C7 corroboration triggers (RG-NOAEL-ALG-13). Each class names compounds where opposite-direction body-weight effects (or other named adversity asymmetries) carry recognized regulatory significance. Citations are primary literature; toxicologist verification recorded in deferred-defect log per AC-F5-5 pattern.
_consumed_by: backend/services/analysis/compound_class.py::resolve_pharmacologic_class (study-class resolution: SME annotation override -> exemplars[] match)
_status: content-complete + WIRED 2026-04-28 (DATA-GAP-NOAEL-ALG-02). All 4 classes (ppar_gamma_agonist, antipsychotic, glucocorticoid, biphasic_hormetic) carry primary citations. Three classes ALSO have full expected-effect profiles in shared/expected-effect-profiles/{ppar_gamma_agonist,antipsychotic,glucocorticoid}.json (biphasic_hormetic has no per-endpoint profile by design -- its signature is dose-response shape, not specific organs). resolve_pharmacologic_class consumes the exemplars[] arrays via case-insensitive substring match against study TS-metadata treatment name; SME annotation override at backend/annotations/{study_id}/compound_profile.json::study.pharmacologic_class wins when set. Cusi/Belfort 2006 NEJM citation amended in commit ecc7efd3 (DATA-GAP-NOAEL-ALG-05 audit). Remaining 7 cited-not-read literature stubs (Nesto 2003, Allison 1999, Schacke 2002, Calabrese 2003/2008/2014, Kale 2022) tracked under DATA-GAP-NOAEL-ALG-05 cleanup.

adverse_signal_classes.rationale: Fluid retention and adipogenesis drive body-weight gain that is regulatorily adverse despite opposing the canonical BW-down adversity direction. Edema is a recognized PPAR-gamma class effect.

citations.source: Belfort R, Harrison SA, Brown K, ..., Cusi K. A placebo-controlled trial of pioglitazone in subjects with nonalcoholic steatohepatitis. New England Journal of Medicine. 2006;355:2297-2307. doi:10.1056/NEJMoa060326
Nesto RW, et al. Thiazolidinedione use, fluid retention, and congestive heart failure. Circulation. 2003;108:2941-2948.
Kale VP, et al. Body weight in toxicologic pathology. Toxicologic Pathology. 2022 [synthesis-cited].

adverse_signal_classes.rationale: Atypical antipsychotics induce metabolic syndrome with body-weight gain, dyslipidemia, and impaired glucose tolerance. BW-up at HD is regulatorily adverse for this class.

citations.source: Allison DB, et al. Antipsychotic-induced weight gain: a comprehensive research synthesis. American Journal of Psychiatry. 1999;156:1686-1696.
Kale VP, et al. Body weight in toxicologic pathology. Toxicologic Pathology. 2022 [synthesis-cited].

adverse_signal_classes.rationale: Glucocorticoids cause body-weight gain via fluid retention, fat redistribution, and metabolic perturbation. BW-up is a recognized adverse pharmacology effect of the class.

citations.source: Schacke H, Docke WD, Asadullah K. Mechanisms involved in the side effects of glucocorticoids. Pharmacology & Therapeutics. 2002;96:23-43.
Kale VP, et al. Body weight in toxicologic pathology. Toxicologic Pathology. 2022 [synthesis-cited].

adverse_signal_classes.rationale: Biphasic / hormetic dose-response patterns produce opposite directions at low vs high dose. Direction-consistency cannot be assumed. C6 suspends; per-pairwise C7 must be evaluated individually with explicit hormesis flag.

citations.source: Calabrese EJ, Baldwin LA. Toxicology rethinks its central belief. Nature. 2003;421:691-692.
Calabrese EJ. Hormesis: principles and applications. Annual Review of Pharmacology and Toxicology. 2008;48:139-160.
Calabrese EJ. Hormesis: a fundamental concept in biology. Microbial Cell. 2014;1:145-149.
